content:
Another thing that stands out is how isolated Phoenix is on this journey. The path represents not only her struggles but also her solitude. It makes her sacrifice feel even more intense because she’s taking on all this responsibility without much help. It adds another layer to her perseverance—she’s not just enduring physical hardship, but emotional isolation as well. This really shows the depth of her sacrifice and why she’s such a powerful character.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 8, 2025 7:15AM
Michelle Herman
I really like how you pointed out the symbolism of the thorn bush and the scarecrow in A Worn Path and how they connect to Phoenix’s perseverance. The thorns are such a strong symbol because they represent all the struggles Phoenix faces, but she doesn't let them stop her. Her comment about the thorns "doing your appointed work" is such an interesting way to look at challenges—like she accepts that difficulties are part of life and just keeps pushing forward. It really shows how determined and strong she is, not just physically, but mentally, as she focuses on her goal despite the obstacles.
